hero_subtitle: SFTY structures case evidence and provider data into audit-ready narratives that support efficient suspicious activity report drafting and streamlined AML compliance workflows.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Narrative drafting complicates suspicious activity review
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to generate clear suspicious activity narratives from dispersed case evidence and fragmented provider outputs. This slows SAR and STR report preparation and weakens audit readiness across AML workflows.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY integrates disparate provider outputs, alerts, reviewer notes and decision logs into a unified workflow that supports compliance teams in generating clear, audit-ready suspicious activity narratives efficiently.
workflow_title: From alerts to suspicious activity reports
workflow_description: SFTY routes alerts into structured cases where reviewers compile evidence, draft suspicious activity narratives and prepare reports for regulatory submission.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented checks. Structured decision control
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects AML alerts, case evidence and regulatory updates into one audit-ready compliance layer without replacing existing provider tools or workflows.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASP compliance teams by preserving audit-ready evidence and enabling provider orchestration within controlled enterprise deployments for defensible suspicious activity narratives.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support suspicious activity narrative drafting
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how case evidence supports SAR and STR drafting
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit-ready records preserve suspicious activity reviews
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe how workflows connect alerts and case escalation
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for AML compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a compliance control layer that connects AML data, alerts, cases, decisions and reports for digital asset compliance teams.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support suspicious activity narratives?
faq_2_answer: SFTY structures case evidence and uses AI-assisted review to help generate clear SAR and STR-style suspicious activity narratives.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case history, decision logs and supporting evidence in audit-ready formats to support defensible compliance and regulatory reviews.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects outputs from KYC, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into a structured workflow without replacing them.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace KYC, KYT or Travel Rule providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY sits above compliance providers and turns their outputs into audit-ready workflows, cases, evidence and reporting.
final_title: Structure suspicious activity narratives with SFTY
final_description: Review how SFTY organizes case evidence and provider data into clear, audit-ready suspicious activity narratives for AML compliance.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
